**Pat Barry** (born July 7, 1979) is an American former professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [kickboxer](/wiki/Kickboxer "Kickboxer") who competed as a [heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight "Heavyweight") in the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). He is particularly known for his [low kicks](/wiki/Low_kick "Low kick").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Barry began practicing [Sanda](/wiki/Sanda_(sport) "Sanda (sport)") while a college student and won numerous titles at domestic and international level before joining [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1") in 2005 where he competed for two years, mostly as a participant on the promotion's North American events.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2008, he embarked on a career in MMA, winning his first three fights by [knockout](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") before signing a contract with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") and competing in the promotion for the next five years as a Heavyweight, compiling a 5–7 record with only one of the matches going [the distance](/wiki/The_distance_(boxing) "The distance (boxing)"). Barry retired from MMA in late 2013 and returned to kickboxing the following year, joining [Glory](/wiki/Glory_(kickboxing) "Glory (kickboxing)").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] He is also the fiancé and training partner of former UFC Women's Strawweight Champion [Rose Namajunas](/wiki/Rose_Namajunas "Rose Namajunas").

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Barry was born in [New Orleans](/wiki/New_Orleans "New Orleans"), Louisiana, to an American military veteran father of European descent and an African-American mother. Barry's parents opened bilingual elementary schools around the world and as a result Barry was raised in [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá"), Colombia until the age of six. When his father died from cancer, he moved back to New Orleans with his mother, older sister and younger brother.

Although he did not start training in martial arts until the age of 21, Barry grew up a fan of ninjas, the *[Street Fighter](/wiki/Street_Fighter "Street Fighter")* video games and [Jean-Claude Van Damme](/wiki/Jean-Claude_Van_Damme "Jean-Claude Van Damme") films and often watched [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1") kickboxing in his youth.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Barry has stated that his three heroes are his mother, [Mike Tyson](/wiki/Mike_Tyson "Mike Tyson") and [Sagat](/wiki/Sagat_(Street_Fighter) "Sagat (Street Fighter)"), a Thai boxer from the *[Street Fighter](/wiki/Street_Fighter "Street Fighter")* video games.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

He worked as a [pizza delivery](/wiki/Pizza_delivery "Pizza delivery") man prior to his career in professional fight sports.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Beginnings in Sanshou (2002–2005)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Beginnings in Sanshou (2002–2005)")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section of a [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **does not [include](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [references or sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). Contentious material about living persons that is unsourced or poorly sourced **must be removed immediately**, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel") or harmful. *(May 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Hailing from [Eastern New Orleans](/wiki/Eastern_New_Orleans "Eastern New Orleans"), Barry started training professionally in [Wushu](/wiki/Wushu_(sport) "Wushu (sport)"), [sanshou](/wiki/Sanshou "Sanshou") and [Kickboxing](/wiki/Kickboxing "Kickboxing") at the age of twenty-one while a junior [sociology](/wiki/Sociology "Sociology") major at the [University of New Orleans](/wiki/University_of_New_Orleans "University of New Orleans") when he walked into Russell Jones's Kickboxing Gym in [Baton Rouge, Louisiana](/wiki/Baton_Rouge,_Louisiana "Baton Rouge, Louisiana"). He made his professional debut on November 2, 2002 at the U.S. Open International Martial Arts Championships in Las Vegas, Nevada, winning the Sanda Kungfu Federation (SKF) United States Heavyweight Championship with a fourth round [knockout](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") over [Robert Parham](/wiki/Robert_Parham "Robert Parham"). He then left Jones' gym and pursued kickboxing full-time with Master [Shawn Liu](/wiki/Shi_Deru "Shi Deru") at LIU International in New Orleans.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In June 2003, Barry fought twice at the *Art of War: China Comes to Atlanta* event in [Atlanta, Georgia](/wiki/Atlanta,_Georgia "Atlanta, Georgia") where he won both a US National Sanshou team spot and the SKF World Heavyweight Championship by knocking out Paul Gurevidius with a high kick. In November 2003, as a [US National Wushu Team](/wiki/United_States_national_wushu_team "United States national wushu team") member, Barry participated at the [2003 World Wushu Championships](/wiki/2003_World_Wushu_Championships "2003 World Wushu Championships") in [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau"), winning a silver medal in the −90 kg/198 lb division. He visited [China](/wiki/China "China") numerous times from 2002 through 2008, amassing over fifty amateur bouts while competing in one of [Shandong](/wiki/Shandong "Shandong")'s stadiums and training with Chinese National Sanshou team at the [Shaolin Temple](/wiki/Shaolin_Temple "Shaolin Temple").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On December 18, 2004, he won the K. Superstar Championship in New Orleans by KOing John Dixson, and a month later in [Gulfport, Mississippi](/wiki/Gulfport,_Mississippi "Gulfport, Mississippi"), he won the *Kings of Kickboxing* tournament by stopping Marshall Berger with [low kicks](/wiki/Low_kick "Low kick") in the final.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### K-1 (2005–2007)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: K-1 (2005–2007)")]

Having taken part in [K-1](/wiki/K-1 "K-1") tryouts held in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") in 2004, Barry was recruited by kickboxing's premier organization and made his promotional debut against [Scott Lighty](/wiki/Scott_Lighty "Scott Lighty") in a tournament reserve bout at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Las Vegas](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2005_in_Las_Vegas "K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Las Vegas")* on April 30, 2005, losing for the first time in his career by [split decision](/wiki/Split_decision "Split decision").[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In June 2005, he met with American kickboxing great [Duke Roufus](/wiki/Duke_Roufus "Duke Roufus") while he was commentating on an event in [Biloxi, Mississippi](/wiki/Biloxi,_Mississippi "Biloxi, Mississippi") and the two became close when Barry stayed with Roufus in [Milwaukee, Wisconsin](/wiki/Milwaukee,_Wisconsin "Milwaukee, Wisconsin") after losing his home in the [Lower Ninth Ward](/wiki/Lower_Ninth_Ward "Lower Ninth Ward") of New Orleans during [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina"),[[9]](#cite_note-9) a tragedy which also claimed the life of his grandmother. After switching to Roufus' gym in Milwaukee, Barry won his first K-1 fight in his sophomore appearance when he knocked out [Mark Selbee](/wiki/Mark_Selbee "Mark Selbee") inside the opening frame at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Las Vegas II](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2005_in_Las_Vegas_II "K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Las Vegas II")* on August 13, 2005.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) Later that month Barry's grandmother was killed in [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

He fought to a [split draw](/wiki/Split_draw "Split draw") with Russian [Kyokushin Karate](/wiki/Kyokushin_kaikan "Kyokushin kaikan") stylist [Aleksandr Pitchkounov](/wiki/Aleksandr_Pitchkounov "Aleksandr Pitchkounov") in a non-tournament bout at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Tokyo Final](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2005_in_Tokyo_Final "K-1 World Grand Prix 2005 in Tokyo Final")* at the [Tokyo Dome](/wiki/Tokyo_Dome "Tokyo Dome") on November 19, 2005. They rematched in the tournament reserve match at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2006 in Las Vegas](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2006_in_Las_Vegas "K-1 World Grand Prix 2006 in Las Vegas")* on April 29, 2006, and Pitchkounov won by split decision.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Following this, Barry moved to [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam") to train under four-time [K-1 World Grand Prix](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix "K-1 World Grand Prix") Champion [Ernesto Hoost](/wiki/Ernesto_Hoost "Ernesto Hoost") at "Team Mr. Perfect" based out of Vos Gym.[[14]](#cite_note-k1wgp-14) Away from K-1, he defeated [Scott Lighty](/wiki/Scott_Lighty "Scott Lighty") in another rematch at *Shin Do Kumate XI* in [Tampa, Florida](/wiki/Tampa,_Florida "Tampa, Florida") on December 8, 2006. After hurting Lighty with low kicks throughout the first two rounds, Barry scored a high kick knockdown at the beginning of the third before finishing him off with another high kick shortly after.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He then returned to K-1 to compete in the eight-man tournament at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2007 in Hawaii](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2007_in_Hawaii "K-1 World Grand Prix 2007 in Hawaii")* on April 4, 2007. He was initially set to face [Jan Nortje](/wiki/Jan_Nortje "Jan Nortje") in the quarter-finals[[15]](#cite_note-15) but his opponent was then changed to [Gary Goodridge](/wiki/Gary_Goodridge "Gary Goodridge").[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Barry twice sent Goodridge to the canvas with low kicks in the early going, the first ruled a slip, the second a legitimate down. He then landed a left high kick on the Trinidadian brawler and, although it did not knock him down, it opened a large cut which caused the ringside physician to call a halt to the bout and send Barry through to the semis where he had his rubber match with Aleksandr Pitchkounov. He was floored with a *[mawashi geri](/wiki/Mawashi_geri "Mawashi geri")* in round two and received a count from the referee in three after being injured when the Russian checked one of his low kicks, losing by [unanimous decision](/wiki/Unanimous_decision "Unanimous decision").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Barry took part in his second K-1 tournament at the *[K-1 World Grand Prix 2007 in Las Vegas](/wiki/K-1_World_Grand_Prix_2007_in_Las_Vegas "K-1 World Grand Prix 2007 in Las Vegas")* on August 11, 2007.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Drawn against [Rickard Nordstrand](/wiki/Rickard_Nordstrand "Rickard Nordstrand") in the quarter-finals, Barry scored a knockdown with a high kick inside the first twenty seconds before stopping the Swede with his patented low kicks in round two. He then went up against [Zabit Samedov](/wiki/Zabit_Samedov "Zabit Samedov") in the semifinals and lost via split decision after a back-and-forth fight.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

In his final K-1 appearance, Barry lost to [Freddy Kemayo](/wiki/Freddy_Kemayo "Freddy Kemayo") via [technical knockout](/wiki/Technical_knockout "Technical knockout") due to a broken nose caused by a [knee strike](/wiki/Knee_(strike) "Knee (strike)") in the opening round of their contest at *[K-1 Fighting Network Prague 2007](/wiki/K-1_Fighting_Network_Prague_2007 "K-1 Fighting Network Prague 2007")* on December 15, 2007.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### World Combat League and transition to mixed martial arts (2008)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: World Combat League and transition to mixed martial arts (2008)")]

In 2008, Barry left Team Mr. Perfect and returned to [Duke Roufus](/wiki/Duke_Roufus "Duke Roufus") at [Roufusport](/wiki/Roufusport "Roufusport") after a fallout with [Ernesto Hoost](/wiki/Ernesto_Hoost "Ernesto Hoost"), as he felt that the Dutch legend neglected him as a trainer and showed little faith in him. In an interview with *Fight!* magazine in 2009, Barry stated:

> Ernesto Hoost just didn't give a fuck about me. He didn't care, man. I was like *hey, I'm leaving, man* and he was like *so?*.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Hoost denied Barry's claims in a 2014 interview, saying that he gave Barry "his all and didn't get a lot back". He also complimented Barry's physical ability but questioned his mental strength.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Barry competed a total of seven times between May and August 2008. Fighting in the [World Combat League](/wiki/World_Combat_League "World Combat League"), a team-based kickboxing competition in which matches are fought over one round, he represented the New York Clash in the -107 kg/235 lb weight class and amassed a record of 3-1 with his only loss coming to a young [Jarrell Miller](/wiki/Jarrell_Miller "Jarrell Miller").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He made his debut in [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") on May 30, 2008 against thirty-eight fight veteran Mike Delaney at *Combat USA: Battle in the Bay 7* in [Green Bay, Wisconsin](/wiki/Green_Bay,_Wisconsin "Green Bay, Wisconsin") and won by TKO in round one after felling his opponent with low kicks.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] He then knocked out John George with a high kick in his next outing at *Combat USA: Fight Night* on June 28, 2008 in [Harris, Michigan](/wiki/Harris,_Michigan "Harris, Michigan"). In his third fight with the promotion, Barry won the Combat USA Heavyweight title when he stopped fellow kickboxer Simon Diouf with low kicks at *Combat USA: Battle in the Bay 8* on August 22, 2008.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He was set to face Andre Walker at *[Strikeforce: Payback](/wiki/Strikeforce:_Payback "Strikeforce: Payback")* in [Broomfield, Colorado](/wiki/Broomfield,_Colorado "Broomfield, Colorado") on October 3, 2008[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26) but instead signed a contract with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") and was replaced by Carlos Zevallos in that bout.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship (2008–2013)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship (2008–2013)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Mirko_Cro_Cop_vs_Pat_Barry_UFC_115.jpg/250px-Mirko_Cro_Cop_vs_Pat_Barry_UFC_115.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mirko_Cro_Cop_vs_Pat_Barry_UFC_115.jpg)

Barry was defeated by [Mirko Cro Cop](/wiki/Mirko_Cro_Cop "Mirko Cro Cop") in the co-main event of *[UFC 115](/wiki/UFC_115 "UFC 115")* in [Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada](/wiki/Vancouver,_British_Columbia,_Canada "Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada") in June 2010.

On December 27, 2008, Barry made his debut in the UFC against [Dan Evensen](/wiki/Dan_Evensen "Dan Evensen") at *[UFC 92](/wiki/UFC_92 "UFC 92")* in Las Vegas,[[28]](#cite_note-28) injuring the Norwegian's left knee with brutal low kicks at 2:36 of the first round thus winning via TKO.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

He then fought UFC newcomer [Tim Hague](/wiki/Tim_Hague "Tim Hague") at *[UFC 98](/wiki/UFC_98 "UFC 98")* on May 23, 2009.[[30]](#cite_note-30) After winning much of the exchanges on the feet, Barry was [taken down](/wiki/Takedown_(grappling) "Takedown (grappling)") by Hague and [submitted](/wiki/Submission_(combat_sports) "Submission (combat sports)") early in the first round with a [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke").[[31]](#cite_note-31)

His next fight was against fellow kickboxer [Antoni Hardonk](/wiki/Antoni_Hardonk "Antoni Hardonk") on October 24, 2009 at *[UFC 104](/wiki/UFC_104 "UFC 104")* in [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California").[[32]](#cite_note-32) After weathering several powerful leg kicks and [punches](/wiki/Punch_(combat) "Punch (combat)") in the first round from Hardonk, Barry found his range in the second round with devastating fast [jab](/wiki/Jab "Jab") combinations, stunning Hardonk several times before dropping him followed by a flurry of punches to finish the fight at 2:30 of the second round.[[33]](#cite_note-33) The win earned him "Knockout of the Night" and "Fight of the Night" honors which brought his total fight purse to [$](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar")134,000.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In a post-fight interview, he claimed to have previously been broke, living on [ketchup](/wiki/Ketchup "Ketchup") and [rice](/wiki/Rice "Rice"), and was six days from being evicted from his home.[[35]](#cite_note-35) In addition to battling heavy financial burden, this fight was also very personal for Barry as he was fighting a member of Ernesto Hoost's training camp. Barry claimed that Hoost had given up on him, resulting in a falling-out between the two and that he waited five years for validation and finally got it when he knocked Hardonk out.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Barry was next scheduled to fight [Gilbert Yvel](/wiki/Gilbert_Yvel "Gilbert Yvel"),[[37]](#cite_note-37) but his opponent was later changed.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

Instead, he fought [PRIDE](/wiki/Pride_Fighting_Championships "Pride Fighting Championships") 2006 Grand Prix champion [Mirko Cro Cop](/wiki/Mirko_Cro_Cop "Mirko Cro Cop") in the co-main event at *[UFC 115](/wiki/UFC_115 "UFC 115")* in [Vancouver](/wiki/Vancouver "Vancouver"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") on June 12, 2010.[[39]](#cite_note-39) Barry dropped and stunned Cro Cop twice in the first round, but ultimately lost the bout via submission due to a [rear-naked choke](/wiki/Rear-naked_choke "Rear-naked choke") in round three.[[40]](#cite_note-40) Barry sustained a broken hand and foot in the fight and he was placed in a wheelchair in the immediate aftermath.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

His next fight was against [Joey Beltran](/wiki/Joey_Beltran "Joey Beltran") at *[UFC: Fight For The Troops 2](/wiki/UFC:_Fight_For_The_Troops_2 "UFC: Fight For The Troops 2")* on January 22, 2011, in [Fort Hood, Texas](/wiki/Fort_Hood,_Texas "Fort Hood, Texas").[[42]](#cite_note-42) Barry used effective leg kicks in the bout to eventually wear down the mobility of Beltran and win a unanimous decision (30–27, 29–28, and 29–28).[[43]](#cite_note-43) At the end of the bout, he jokingly "fired" [Matt Mitrione](/wiki/Matt_Mitrione "Matt Mitrione"). This was likely a joke based on Mitrione firing his agent publicly after a previous fight.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Barry fought fellow striker [Cheick Kongo](/wiki/Cheick_Kongo "Cheick Kongo") on June 26, 2011 in the main event of *[UFC on Versus 4](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_4 "UFC on Versus 4")* in [Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pittsburgh,_Pennsylvania "Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania") on June 26, 2011[[44]](#cite_note-44) and lost the fight in stunning fashion. Early in the first round Barry dropped and stunned Kongo, and delivered a flurry of strikes. Kongo weathered the assault and, still on wobbly legs, caught Barry with an [overhand](/wiki/Overhand_(boxing) "Overhand (boxing)") right on the ear followed by an [uppercut](/wiki/Uppercut "Uppercut") to the chin, knocking Pat out cold.[[45]](#cite_note-45) This was the first KO loss of Barry's career in both kickboxing and MMA.

Barry faced the tallest fighter in the UFC [Stefan Struve](/wiki/Stefan_Struve "Stefan Struve") on October 1, 2011 at *[UFC on Versus 6](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_6 "UFC on Versus 6")* in [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.")[[46]](#cite_note-46) Barry was winning most of the striking exchanges midway into the second round but in a turn of events, Barry took Struve down to the mat and Struve locked in a [triangle choke](/wiki/Triangle_choke "Triangle choke"). Barry slammed Struve but left his arm exposed and Struve was able to secure an [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar"). Barry lost by triangle armbar submission at 3:22 of the second round.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

2011 also saw Barry leave Roufusport for Team DeathClutch in [Alexandria, Minnesota](/wiki/Alexandria,_Minnesota "Alexandria, Minnesota").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Barry faced highly touted brawler [Christian Morecraft](/wiki/Christian_Morecraft "Christian Morecraft") on January 20, 2012 at *[UFC on FX 1](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_1 "UFC on FX 1")* in [Nashville, Tennessee](/wiki/Nashville,_Tennessee "Nashville, Tennessee").[[48]](#cite_note-48) Despite being caught early in an armbar, Barry escaped the submission attempt and won the fight via KO at 3:38 of the first round.[[49]](#cite_note-49) His win over Morecraft earned him *Fight of The Night* honors for the second time in his career.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

On May 5, 2012, Barry faced the heavy-handed [Lavar Johnson](/wiki/Lavar_Johnson "Lavar Johnson") at *[UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3")* in [East Rutherford, New Jersey](/wiki/East_Rutherford,_New_Jersey "East Rutherford, New Jersey")[[51]](#cite_note-51) and lost via TKO in the first round. Despite the loss, Barry was able to get a takedown during the fight, and even attempted a [keylock](/wiki/Keylock "Keylock") submission. However, Johnson worked his way to his feet, trapped Barry against the cage, and began to tee off with punches until Barry dropped to the floor and the referee intervened to stop the fight.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

Following Team DeathClutch's closure, Barry briefly moved to Greg Nelson's [Minnesota Martial Arts Academy](/wiki/Minnesota_Martial_Arts_Academy "Minnesota Martial Arts Academy") in [Minneapolis, Minnesota](/wiki/Minneapolis,_Minnesota "Minneapolis, Minnesota") in October 2012[[53]](#cite_note-53) before relocating to [Arvada, Colorado](/wiki/Arvada,_Colorado "Arvada, Colorado") to train under [Trevor Wittman](/wiki/Trevor_Wittman "Trevor Wittman") at the [Grudge Training Center](/wiki/Grudge_Training_Center "Grudge Training Center").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Barry faced former [Muay Thai](/wiki/Muay_Thai "Muay Thai") Champion [Shane del Rosario](/wiki/Shane_del_Rosario "Shane del Rosario") at *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Carwin vs. Team Nelson Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Carwin_vs._Team_Nelson_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Carwin vs. Team Nelson Finale")* on December 15, 2012.[[54]](#cite_note-54) After a close first round where Barry was able to escape from some submission attempts, he connected with a huge left hand followed with a flurry of punches, finished by a devastating right hook, and won by KO at 0:26 of round two, earning *Knockout of The Night* honors for the second time.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

He faced [Shawn Jordan](/wiki/Shawn_Jordan "Shawn Jordan") on June 15, 2013 at *[UFC 161](/wiki/UFC_161 "UFC 161")*, losing by TKO in round one. However, later reports came that the stoppage might have been caused by an eye poke from Jordan. The report showed that the uppercut Jordan landed (which seemed to daze Barry) wasn't an uppercut but an open hand blow. The report also showed that Jordan's fingers found their way into Barry's eyes. Following the [uppercut](/wiki/Uppercut "Uppercut"), Barry was trying to protect the eye from the next of Jordan's punches. Barry did not mention that he had been poked in the eye, but pictures from the day after the fight show that he tapped to strikes three times.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

In what would be his 12th and final UFC appearance, Barry was knocked out with vicious [ground-and-pound](/wiki/Ground-and-pound "Ground-and-pound") from the [mount](/wiki/Mount_(grappling) "Mount (grappling)") by [Soa Palelei](/wiki/Soa_Palelei "Soa Palelei") on December 7, 2013 at *[UFC Fight Night 33](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_33 "UFC Fight Night 33")* in [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia").[[57]](#cite_note-57)

### Return to kickboxing; Glory, and independent promotions (2014–2015)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Return to kickboxing; Glory, and independent promotions (2014–2015)")]

On January 28, 2014, Barry announced his retirement from MMA via [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter")[[58]](#cite_note-58)[[59]](#cite_note-59) and his manager subsequently stated he would likely return to kickboxing.[[60]](#cite_note-60) He appeared on [Ariel Helwani](/wiki/Ariel_Helwani "Ariel Helwani")'s show the following month and announced that he had signed for world's top promotion, *[Glory](/wiki/Glory_(kickboxing) "Glory (kickboxing)")*.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

In his first outing since returning to kickboxing, a warm-up fight ahead of his Glory debut, Barry defeated domestic heavyweight Ed Burris at *Combat Sports Challenge 39* in [Richmond, Virginia](/wiki/Richmond,_Virginia "Richmond, Virginia") on March 22, 2014,[[62]](#cite_note-62) scoring a knockdown with a bodyshot in round one before stopping Burris with a flurry of punches in round two.[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)[[65]](#cite_note-65)

He lost to [Zack Mwekassa](/wiki/Zack_Mwekassa "Zack Mwekassa") in the *[Glory 16: Denver - Heavyweight Contendership Tournament](/wiki/Glory_16:_Denver_-_Heavyweight_Contendership_Tournament "Glory 16: Denver - Heavyweight Contendership Tournament")* reserve match in Broomfield on May 3, 2014,[[66]](#cite_note-66) suffering an early knockdown before being put away with a left uppercut towards the end of round one.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

Just a day after this loss, it was reported that Barry was scheduled to rematch Mirko Cro Cop at *[Glory 17: Los Angeles](/wiki/Glory_17:_Los_Angeles "Glory 17: Los Angeles")* in [Inglewood, California](/wiki/Inglewood,_California "Inglewood, California") on June 21, 2014.[[68]](#cite_note-68) He withdrew from the fight for undisclosed reasons, however, and was replaced by [Sergei Kharitonov](/wiki/Sergei_Kharitonov "Sergei Kharitonov").[[69]](#cite_note-69)

Still under contract with [Glory](/wiki/Glory_(kickboxing) "Glory (kickboxing)"), Barry had his next fight in a different organization when he took on Demoreo Dennis at Legacy FC's first kickboxing event on January 16, 2015.[[70]](#cite_note-70)[[71]](#cite_note-71)[[72]](#cite_note-72)

After an even first round, Barry started to land heavy leg kicks, from which he landed a knockdown in round 2, and punches, as well as using a good defense against his opponents strikes. Barry got the best of it for the last two rounds, earning the decision victory.[[73]](#cite_note-73)

Barry was expected to return to Glory and face veteran [Mourad Bouzidi](/wiki/Mourad_Bouzidi "Mourad Bouzidi") on April 3, 2015 at [Glory 20](/wiki/Glory_20 "Glory 20").[[74]](#cite_note-74)[[75]](#cite_note-75) However, Barry was forced out of the bout due to an injured hand and was replaced by [Dustin Jacoby](/wiki/Dustin_Jacoby "Dustin Jacoby").[[76]](#cite_note-76)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pat_Barry_(fighter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Barry is the fiancé and training partner of former UFC Women's Strawweight Champion [Rose Namajunas](/wiki/Rose_Namajunas "Rose Namajunas").[[77]](#cite_note-77)[[78]](#cite_note-78)[[79]](#cite_note-79) Namajunas and Barry met at [Duke Roufus' gym](/wiki/Roufusport "Roufusport"), where Barry started training after losing his home to [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina") in 2005, while Namajunas started training there during high school.[[80]](#cite_note-80)
Barry says he fell in love with Namajunas the first time he saw her. In a December 2014 interview, Barry said that he and Namajunas had been dating "for almost five years now", while Namajunas felt like it was "two or three years". The couple got engaged in 2014.[[81]](#cite_note-81)

Following their fight at *[UFC 115](/wiki/UFC_115 "UFC 115")* in June 2010, Barry and [Mirko Cro Cop](/wiki/Mirko_Cro_Cop "Mirko Cro Cop") have developed a friendship and Barry frequently trains with Cro Cop at his training center in [Zagreb, Croatia](/wiki/Zagreb,_Croatia "Zagreb, Croatia").[[82]](#cite_note-82)[[83]](#cite_note-83)

In August 2017, Barry claimed to have been one year sober after many years of alcohol and drug abuse. In his own words, he said that his daily routine after professional fighting had become "straight vodka, painkillers and cocaine for breakfast".[[84]](#cite_note-84)
